text: Writing clean code is essential for any programmer who wants their work to be easy to understand and maintain. Readable code is like well-written prose; it clearly communicates its purpose. A key part of this is using good naming conventions. Variable and function names should describe what they do, for example, `calculateTotalPrice()` instead of `calc()`. Furthermore, keep your functions short and focused on a single task. This makes each part of your code simpler to test and reuse. Ultimately, clean code saves time, reduces errors, and makes collaboration with other developers much smoother.
